text: Morgan Stanley's Morgan Stanley Bitcoin Trust(MSBT)recorded $193.6 million in cumulative net inflows and $239.6 million in net assets from its April 8 launch to May 7, with 17 days of inflows, five flat days and no daily net outflows. text: Highlight Clip Strategy CEO: Bitcoin Will Only Be Sold Under Two Conditions On May 9, 2025, Strategy CEO Phong Le addressed the company's strategic pivot from a "never sell" stance to actively managing its Bitcoin holdings during an interview with CNBC. Le expressed a "math over ideology" approach, stating that the company will only sell Bitcoin under specific conditions: first, to pay dividends for STRC (perpetual preferred stock) when it is more accretive to "Bitcoin per share" than issuing new equity; and second, for tax optimization purposes, such as capturing or deferring tax gains and losses. — link

text: Navigating the meta machine What NOT to do - Refusing to accept that a failed meta is dead. Whether it's your attention or capital, you need to cut your losses. This opens the space to participate in the next meta - Believing a new meta will fail because a previous variant of it did. The longer you've been in crypto, the more likely this affects you. E.g. many long-time crypto participants concluded "nobody cares about privacy" and have consequently been sidelined for the resurgence of ZEC - Rejecting a new meta because it's too wonky or divergent from the current meta. You have to be able to extrapolate the potential of a meta from first principles versus just comping it against the existing metas - Ignoring a new meta because it competes with an existing meta that you successfully mastered.

text: The task-completion time horizon is the task duration (measured by human expert completion time) at which an AI agent is predicted to succeed with a given level of reliability. For example, the 50%-time horizon is the duration at which an agent is predicted to succeed half the time. The graph below shows the 50%- and 80%-time horizons for frontier AI agents, calculated using their performance on over a hundred diverse software tasks. METR evaluated an early Claude Mythos Preview in March 2026 and estimated a 50% time-horizon of at least 16 hours (95% CI 8.5-55 hours) on their task suite, placing it at the upper measurable limit Only 5 of 228 tasks exceed 16 hours, making high-end estimates unstable; METR cautions against precise comparisons or extrapolations at this range and notes they are developing longer tasks

text: CME Targets June 1 Launch for Bitcoin Volatility Futures, Pending CFTC Review CME Group plans to launch Bitcoin Volatility Futures(BVI)on June 1, 2026, pending CFTC review. The contract size will be $500 multiplied by the CME CF Bitcoin Volatility Index, which measures 30-day implied volatility using real-time order book data from CME Bitcoin and Micro Bitcoin options. CME said the product will allow traders to go long or short Bitcoin volatility without taking direct directional exposure to BTC, providing a new risk management tool for institutional investors. — link

text: Highlight Clip Vitalik Signals "ZK Payments" as Next Standard for the Agent Era Vitalik Buterin stated that at the protocol level for native agent standards, the next step for payments is likely ZK Payments. He believes that in AI scenarios, persistent pseudonymous identities will eventually lose all privacy. The team is developing a ZK API to isolate each request, which protects privacy without requiring an on-chain transaction for every single action, and can be combined with security deposits to prevent abuse. — link

text: Strategy CEO Says BTC Sales Would Only Happen Under Specific Conditions Strategy CEO Phong Le told CNBC the firm will only sell Bitcoin under specific conditions, such as funding the 11.5% dividend on its STRC preferred stock or for tax optimization. Le stressed that the company weighs selling BTC against issuing stock to prioritize its "Bitcoin per Share" metric, ensuring any sale is strictly accretive to common shareholders. This clarifies Executive Chairman Michael Saylor's recent remark about potentially selling Bitcoin to pay dividends, which had sparked market speculation. Forbes contributor and Two Prime CEO Alexander S. Blume highlighted that Michael Saylor's recent openness to selling Bitcoin under specific conditions breaks his core "never sell" promise. Blume noted that Strategy's investment products lack direct BTC claims, forcing the firm to balance USD debt servicing with its BTC accumulation narrative. He cautioned that frequent narrative shifts could erode investor trust, potentially triggering a cascading sell-off across MSTR, STRC, and BTC itself. — link

text: $2B in Protocol TVL Moves From LayerZero to Chainlink CCIP Analyst Tom Wan notes that despite LayerZero's apology, protocols with ~$2 billion in combined TVL—including KelpDAO ($1.5B), SolvProtocol ($600M), and re ($200M)—are migrating to Chainlink CCIP. However, major assets like Ethena's USDe, Etherfi's weETH, and Bitgo's WBTC still utilize LayerZero's OFT standard. This exodus follows LayerZero's apology for poor communication during a recent exploit. The team admitted their internal RPC was attacked by the Lazarus Group and confessed to a 1/1 DVN misconfiguration that created a critical single point of failure. — link

text: BitGo CEO Pushes Back Against Report Warning Quantum Computing Could Threaten BTC Wallets by 2030 CoinDesk reported that post-quantum security firm Project Eleven warned quantum computing could threaten BTC wallet security by 2030. The firm said Bitcoin's bigger challenge is not technical, but coordination: users, exchanges, custodians and miners would all need to migrate to post-quantum cryptography at the same time. BitGo CEO Mike Belshe pushed back, arguing that the warning comes from a company whose business model depends on public concern over quantum computing. — link
